items: Prepared for {{fact:f:E-2001-001.client}}
Progress briefing to the client
Brackenridge Civil Group Inc
Engagement commenced {{fact:f:E-2001-001.start}}
Presented by Linda Nguyen, Design Technician
A sealed grading plan set, suitable for agency submittal and for construction
Finished floor and pavement elevations, cut and fill limits, slopes, spot elevations
Stormwater conveyance sized to serve the graded site, with supporting computations
Erosion and sediment control plan and details
Earthwork quantity estimate for the graded condition
Topographic survey and geotechnical report received and checked against the field before drawing began
Two disagreements between the survey and the existing conditions were written up and resolved rather than worked around
Outfall confirmed with the reviewing agency by telephone, and the conveyance layout established
Drainage computations under way, prepared by Jason Jordan against the method the agency accepts
Preliminary grading in progress; it comes to you for review before any sheet is developed in detail
Drainage first: the outfall and the conveyance are set before the finished contours are drawn
Contours are fitted to the conveyance, not the reverse, which is what keeps the earthwork honest
Preliminary grading comes to you for review while a change is still inexpensive to make
Interim prints are issued for review and are not for construction
Only the sealed cover sheet governs; nothing is built from a check print
Finished floor elevation at the north building: accept the imported fill, or shift the pad
Cross slope at the loading area, which governs the cut along the east side; the two cannot be traded independently
Confirmation that the utility layout is final; the grading and the utilities govern one another at three locations
Direction from Debra Vasquez on the above would let the preliminary grading be issued for review
Preliminary grading to Esparza-Lamb for review once the pad elevations are confirmed
Drainage computations completed and checked against the method the agency accepts
Erosion and sediment control plan developed alongside the grading, not after it
Utility coordination meeting to close the three points where the layouts conflict
Plan set assembly to begin after the preliminary grading has been accepted
